rationale.R1.1: The section constructs a specific gap narrative — 'prevailing view requires impractically large noise, we challenge this' — which must be checked for accuracy and internal coherence as a contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain (large-noise consensus → O(1/d^2) step-size bounds → CA-SGD escapes in O(log(1/ε)) at first-order cost → Lyapunov proof) contains multiple technical premises whose logical dependencies need verification.
rationale.R1.4: The section asserts 'first first-order method to achieve this rate without large noise injection' — a load-bearing novelty claim that review must evaluate against known literature.
rationale.R1.7: The section characterizes 'the current consensus' and 'the prevailing view' with no citations; reviewing requires checking whether those characterizations fairly represent the literature and whether attribution is complete.

parsed.clarification_question: Is this a technical/content review (checking logical soundness of the argument chain, validity of the 'first first-order method' novelty claim, and accuracy of prior-work characterizations) or a writing/prose review (improving sentence clarity, style, and readability)?
